pi_items.source: Consistent across task.json, task description, setup_task.sh (line 6), and export_result.sh (line 12)

Calculated from wind turbine physics: 10m blade × 1m chord × 3 blades at ~15 m/s wind speed yields ~19,500-20,000 Nm root flapwise bending moment. QBlade outputs root bending moments in Nm for structural analysis.

Calculated from wind turbine physics: 10m blade at 25 m/s wind speed with constant 1m chord yields ~54,000-60,000 Nm root flapwise bending moment based on thrust = 0.5×ρ×V²×A×Ct formula with typical Ct~0.8
Stated in task description: 'Length=10m' and consistent in task.json metadata
Stated in task description: 'Fixed RPM=60' and consistent in task.json metadata
Stated in task description: 'Range 5-25 m/s' and consistent in task.json metadata

privileged_info_summary: This is a QBlade wind turbine simulation task. The task requires designing a NACA 0012 airfoil (100 panels) at Re=1,000,000, creating a simple rectangular blade (10m length, 1m chord, 0.5m hub radius, 3 blades total), running a Wind Speed Sweep BEM simulation from 5-25 m/s at fixed 60 RPM and 0° pitch, and extracting the maximum root flapwise bending moment (My) from the results. The expected root bending moment range of 20,000-60,000 Nm is physically plausible for this blade configuration across the specified wind speed range, with lower moments (~20,000 Nm) at intermediate wind speeds (~15 m/s) and higher moments (~54,000-60,000 Nm) at maximum wind speed (25 m/s). The output file must be written to /home/ga/Documents/projects/root_moment_report.txt containing only the integer moment value in Nm.
